findings.fix: Three moves, cheapest first: (1) put display strings in the frozen tray (data is frozen, so "63:36" is data, not render logic) — this deletes the per-paradigm formatting surface entirely; (2) make the committed fixture adversarially branch-covering by construction (null duration, >=1h track, single-format release, non-square cover, 0-and-5-thumb galleries, NBSP/U+2153 text) and record the branch checklist; (3) add a required LOCAL pre-publish drift run pointing the gate at crate-built variants (local reads the crate fine) before any number publishes.

findings.claim_attacked: Canonical checkout markup includes "Order summary (cart lines + totals)" and the shared masthead carries a "cart link with count slot" — with golden masters that "render from tray JSON".
findings.evidence: ADR-0004:110 is binding: "localStorage holds the cart only" — client-only state, so NO variant's served DOM can ever contain real cart lines or a count. Fork: if the checkout master renders fixture cart lines (tray JSON has no cart anyway), every variant fails the DOM gate forever (normalize.ts:40-41 exact-match); if the master renders the empty-cart skeleton, the populated summary and count are post-hydration DOM the gate never checks — normalize.ts:66-68: "a hydrating variant's post-hydration mutations are unchecked until the JS-on second pass lands". The draft specifies neither.
findings.why_it_matters: The served-DOM contract for every cart-dependent region is currently unwritable, and whichever default gets coded first either bricks the gate or silently exempts checkout's most visible region from the drift guarantee.
findings.fix: Pin it in the draft/ADR: canonical served DOM = the empty-cart state (empty count slot, summary skeleton with reserved dimensions); populated cart states are explicitly declared per-paradigm behavior, ungated until the JS-on pass, with the count slot width-reserved so client population can't shift layout.

findings.claim_attacked: "Live mini-readout: the two most legible vitals stream in via measure.js" on a chrome whose sketch reads "CLS 0.00" — with no width-reservation design; plus a growing render-blocking chrome payload.
findings.evidence: client.ts:35-39 writes textContent into the live slots as each metric settles; chrome.css:19-24 lays the row out flex-wrap and chrome.css:71-74 gives .pm-chrome__metric-value no min-width — replacing "–" with "654ms" widens the span and shifts siblings (possibly rewrapping the strip) seconds after load, with no qualifying recent input, so the SAME onCLS (client.ts:56) that feeds the beacon counts the instrument's own shifts on every measured page. Separately: index.js:80-84 injects chrome.ts:93's `<link href="/_pm/chrome.css">` into a body slot the draft places ABOVE the masthead (draft line 148) — excluded from measured KB (collect.ts:91-95) but not from the throttled wire or render-blocking path, and the redesign grows it (panel styles + forced-colors block + a new self-hosted mono).
findings.why_it_matters: "Your instrument manufactures the CLS it reports" is a one-tweet debunk of "zero CLS by construction"; and the hero-chrome growth silently raises the constant wall-clock tax on every variant's FCP/LCP that ADR-0004:187 justified when the chrome was 75 lines of CSS.
findings.fix: Design the reservation into the component spec: ch-fixed metric-value widths (tabular figures already guarantee stable digits), fixed collapsed-row height, em-dash placeholder same width class; set an explicit chrome.css+font byte budget and a font-display strategy for the mono; publish a measured before/after of the chrome tax so the "constant, not a variable" argument is a receipt, not a claim.

findings.claim_attacked: "Empty state (ships now, C2): the table renders with all cells em-dash" while "each surface's variants array stays empty until a variant build actually serves it" and "the planned lineups live in the ADR table".
findings.evidence: The lab table's "columns = the variants this surface is built in (sparse)" (draft lines 76-78) has ZERO columns when variants is empty — there are no cells to render em-dashes into; the column source the empty state needs lives only in a doc (draft lines 318-320), unreachable by the renderer. Worse, chrome.ts:99-101 falls back to `"singleton surface"` whenever a surface has no registered variants — so on day one PDP, PLP, and Checkout would all be labeled singleton, which is false (they are planned multi-variant, just unbuilt).
findings.why_it_matters: The two sections contradict each other, and the shipped mechanism resolves the contradiction in the worst direction: the chrome renders a wrong statement ("singleton") instead of the designed empty state, on every surface, for the entire pre-variant period.
findings.fix: Grow the typed SurfaceControls shape with `plannedVariants` (rendered as dead, non-anchor column headers labeled "not built yet") distinct from `variants` (live anchors). Sparse honesty holds — a dead labeled cell is a disclosure, not an offer — and the em-dash table becomes renderable from config on day one.

findings.claim_attacked: "Thumbnails reuse the gallery images (same URLs — browser cache, zero extra bytes)".
findings.evidence: Images 2-5 appear nowhere else on the PDP: the thumbnail row is their FIRST fetch, at full 600px derivative transferSize, and collect.ts:230 books every non-instrumentation resource-timing entry into the buckets — so the receipt's images bucket carries ~4 full-size images the visitor may never enlarge. Only thumb #1 (same URL as the main figure) is genuinely zero extra transfer. These fetches are above the fold, so loading="lazy" won't defer them, and on the Slow 4G profile they contend with the fetchpriority="high" LCP image.
findings.why_it_matters: The claim is falsifiable by the project's own receipt (image-bucket bytes), and eager full-size thumbnails are kill-condition-1 surface: "of course it's slow — the page front-loads five product shots" — identical across paradigms, so not bias, but it taxes every absolute number and the claim as written reads as bookkeeping sleight-of-hand.
findings.fix: Restate honestly: "no thumb derivative set is minted; SWAPPING costs zero additional bytes; the thumbnail row itself costs N full-size fetches, identical in every variant and counted in every receipt." Then decide eager-vs-alternatives with a measured LCP-contention check on Slow 4G before fixing the markup contract.

findings.claim_attacked: "a fixed aspect frame (1:1, the dominant crate shape — 1,022 of 1,817 at 600×600)".
findings.evidence: Tool-verified against tools/snapshot-capture/crate/details.json: 653 of 1,817 gallery images are exactly 600×600 (not 1,022); 771/1,817 (42.4%) are square at all; landscape is 676 and portrait 370. Among the 500 covers (the default main-figure population) 296 (59.2%) are square. The frame's swap population is ALL gallery images, so the MAJORITY of swapped-in images letterbox.
findings.why_it_matters: The stat is typed, wrong on both numbers, and cited as the design's justification — in a draft whose own §4.3 rule is "numbers... come from tray/manifest fields — never typed (ADR-0007's lesson)". A panelist or reader with jq falsifies it in one line.
findings.fix: Correct the figures (296/500 covers square is the honest support for a 1:1 default frame; 42.4% square overall is the honest letterbox rate), cite the covers population explicitly, and derive the numbers in the doc build from the crate rather than typing them. The fixed-frame zero-shift mechanism itself survives — keep it.

findings.claim_attacked: "Every component is expressible idiomatically in vanilla, React, Astro, Qwik, and HTMX with byte-identical DOM."
findings.evidence: Idiomatic HTMX and Qwik put load-bearing attributes IN the served HTML (hx-get/hx-target on the sort form and gallery thumbs; Qwik's on:*/q:* on every interactive element) — these must ride the PERMITTED_NOISE registry (normalize.ts:69-75) to pass the gate, but ADR-0003 §6 enumerates the permitted classes as "hydration markers, comment nodes, and the scoping hashes" — behavior attributes are semantics, not noise. View-source on any HTMX page falsifies "byte-identical" as literally stated.
findings.why_it_matters: The kill condition is the draft's own headline control; overclaiming it hands a skeptic the cheapest possible discount ("the allowlist means it's not byte-identical, so what else is allowlisted?"). The registry design is sound — the phrasing isn't.
findings.fix: State the precise claim: "identical modulo a per-variant, audited noise registry (one file, PERMITTED_NOISE)" — and decide now whether behavior attributes (hx-*, on:*, q:*) get their own declared registry class distinct from inert noise, since they are the paradigm's mechanism, not its residue.

findings.claim_attacked: "the chrome carries NO mode toggles — the demos own them in-page".
findings.evidence: ADR-0004:144 assigns "a11y-mode toggles on the A11y section" to the contextual switcher's control-set. The draft reverses this (with a better argument — chrome must stay byte-identical while toggles are page-state) but never names it as a reversal.
findings.why_it_matters: A binding ADR contradicted silently is exactly the drift-in-docs the project polices elsewhere.
findings.fix: One line in the coming ADR: "amends ADR-0004 §7 — a11y-mode toggles move in-page; rationale: chrome byte-identity + emulation-honesty caveat colocation."

findings.claim_attacked: "the first row eager (LCP candidates)" for PLP grid images.
findings.evidence: Row membership is breakpoint-dependent (2-up mobile vs 4-up desktop) but loading attributes are static markup fixed by the canonical contract — "first row" is unimplementable as written.
findings.why_it_matters: The markup contract must pin an exact eager count or five builds will pick five different ones — which IS DOM drift under normalize.ts:40-41.
findings.fix: Pin the number in the contract (e.g., first 4 images eager, rest lazy) and say why.

findings.claim_attacked: Checkout is the only surface without a stated JS-off story (PDP gets one at draft lines 175-176).
findings.evidence: With the cart in localStorage (ADR-0004:110) and validation/summary-recalc being the measured JS work, JS-off checkout is an empty summary plus a submit that goes nowhere — unstated in the draft.
findings.why_it_matters: Every other surface pre-empts the honesty question; leaving checkout's silent invites "what happens JS-off?" as a gotcha on the surface whose thesis is JS load.
findings.fix: Add the one-line JS-off statement using the same "interactivity earns JS" honesty framing PDP uses.

survived: 1.1 top-strip in-flow placement: the pixel gate already handles an in-flow chrome by removal+reflow (gate.ts:69-83) — no body restyle needed, rejected placements correctly rejected

1.2 chrome-owned mono: /_pm/fonts rides the instrumentation boundary and is genuinely excluded from measured KB (collect.ts:91-95); ADR-0006:130 does leave the chrome-CSS route open exactly as the draft quotes
1.4 injection contract: matches the shipped worker verbatim (workers/front/src/index.js:80-95, chrome.ts:93-109), slot-cardinality logging included

2.2 form-state DOM (checked radios, value on qty, aria-pressed): the gate compares the browser-PARSED served DOM JS-off (gate.ts:38, normalize.ts:106 clones the parsed document), so boolean-attribute and entity-escaping serialization differences between paradigms vanish at parse — no new normalizer permission needed for the served DOM
1.5 plaques as page bytes in the store face: correct side of the instrumentation boundary; shipping the chrome mono into plaques would indeed tax measured KB
4.1 glyph subset + fontkit cmap re-derivation guard: closes the coverage.json hand-fake hole
4.2 first half (600px derivative ceiling): the sizing arithmetic holds — originals cap near 600, so no variant could be advantaged by a derivative that doesn't exist upstream
2.4 no-artificial-busy-loops INP design: the interaction registry work is real checkout work and survives view-source

findings.claim_attacked: Shared shell masthead with nav 'Records / Editorial / Checkout' as canonical markup on all store surfaces (DRAFT.md:144-148)
findings.evidence: The sparse matrix makes this shell unbuildable as canonical markup: decision-map.md:31 — PLP is 'Built in: React/Next + HTMX' only; decision-map.md:32 — Checkout is 'vanilla, React/Next, HTMX (Qwik opt)'. So on /vanilla/pdp/... the masthead's 'Records' link has no same-variant target (vanilla serves no PLP), and on /astro/... 'Checkout' has none. ADR-0003 §1 (0003:37-48) requires every variant to emit identical DOM per surface, and the drift gate compares the whole page minus ONLY the chrome slot (tools/drift-gate/src/normalize.ts:107 `root.querySelectorAll("div#pm-chrome-slot")` — the masthead is page bytes, diffed). ADR-0004 §7 (0004:145-148) enshrines 'the switcher can never offer a cell that does not exist'; the draft applies that to the switcher (§1.4) but ships a masthead that offers cells that don't exist.
findings.why_it_matters: As drafted the store shell must either ship 404 links on most measured pages (a credibility kill on 'the page a performance-obsessed senior engineer would build') or emit per-variant nav DOM, which fails the drift gate / violates the binding canonical-markup contract.
findings.fix: Decide the mechanism before build and record it in the ADR. Options: (a) masthead carries brand + cart only; cross-surface navigation lives in the chrome/home gateway (cheapest, zero new contract); (b) variant-relative hrefs kept identical + the front Worker 307s an unbuilt {variant}/{surface} to a variant that serves it (DOM identity preserved; the redirect silently swaps variants — must be labeled); (c) nav hrefs pinned to each surface's designated host variant (honest, but a link from /vanilla/ into /react-next/ is a variant swap outside the switcher and needs the same labeling). Each option's tradeoff should be argued in the ADR.

findings.claim_attacked: 'correct by data: Discogs originals cap near 600px, so 600 is the ceiling regardless' and '1:1, the dominant crate shape — 1,022 of 1,817 at 600×600' (DRAFT.md:296-304, 192-194)
findings.evidence: Both numbers fail against the committed crate. (1) jq over tools/snapshot-capture/crate/details.json: exactly 600×600 = 653 of 1,817 (images-index.json agrees: 653); exact square any size = 771; no tolerance sweep (|w−h| ≤ 1..12, aspect ±2%) produces 1,022. (2) 'originals cap near 600px' is unverifiable from committed data: tools/snapshot-capture/src/derive.ts:44 `.resize({ width: 600, height: 600, fit: "inside", withoutEnlargement: true })` censors every derivative at 600 — 1,196/1,817 derivatives sit AT the ceiling, so their originals are only known to be ≥600; images-index.json records originalSha256 but no original dimensions. Issue #9's follow-up (comment, item 6) says sizing 'may be refined when aesthetic-direction/the PDP build fix final dimensions — originals are retained, so re-derivation never touches Discogs' — retained originals make the claim checkable in one local pass, and the draft doesn't take it.
findings.why_it_matters: §4.2 closes a tracked GitHub follow-up with the phrase 'correct by data' on one number that is false and one that is unproven — the exact failure ADR-0007's method section memorializes (the panel catching a hand-typed SHA 'is how a wrong receipt ships'). If this lands in the issue-closure comment it is a published wrong receipt on a receipts-credibility project.
findings.fix: Before closing #9: run a one-command dimension scan over the retained originals and record the distribution in the closure comment; replace 1,022 with the tool-derived count (653 at exactly 600×600 / 771 square / 1,196 at the ceiling) and restate the 1:1-dominance claim from the main-figure population (296/500 first images exactly square; 454/500 within 2%). If originals genuinely cap near 600, the closure stands — with the receipt attached.

findings.claim_attacked: 'the chrome carries NO mode toggles — the demos own them in-page' (DRAFT.md:105-108) vs ADR-0004 §7's decided control-set
findings.evidence: ADR-0004 §7 (0004:142-144) is a Decision clause: 'device/CPU controls on Checkout, a11y-mode toggles on the A11y section' — echoed in decision-map.md:37. The draft reverses the a11y allocation without an addendum. Its stated rationale partly misfires: 'the chrome must stay byte-identical while toggles are page-state' — §7's byte-identity is ACROSS VARIANTS by construction (0004:133-135), and the a11y section is a single-variant singleton, so that clause isn't threatened. The load-bearing argument is elsewhere in the same §7: 'JS enhances only the HUD's live readout' (0004:139-141) — a chrome-hosted emulation toggle is impossible without new chrome JS mutating page state across the instrumentation/store boundary, or a URL knob. The a11y-section ticket (decision-map.md:198) already assigns 'mode-emulation primitives' to the DS/page side, supporting the draft.
findings.why_it_matters: Silently contradicting an accepted ADR's letter is exactly what this project's record exists to prevent; and the draft's weaker rationale invites a reviewer to reject the right decision for the wrong reason.
findings.fix: Keep the page-owned toggles, but (a) record an explicit ADR-0004 §7 amendment in the new surface-design ADR grounded in §7's own JS clause, (b) name the rejected alternative — mode as a query-param anchor (JS-off-clean, chrome-hostable) — and why it loses (emulation state is not a measurement condition and would pollute URL-as-receipt), and (c) fix the byte-identical justification in the draft.

findings.claim_attacked: Chrome-owned mono @font-face in chrome.css, with the strip in-flow at the top of every measured page (DRAFT.md:44-53, 31-34)
findings.evidence: The route itself is authorized (ADR-0006 alternatives, 0006:129-132: the mono 'remains reachable later through the `--pm-font-metric` slot and chrome CSS'; ADR-0003 §8 even names 'tabular/mono figures for metrics (aligned prices, HUD)'). Two unaddressed mechanisms: (1) CLS — the draft claims 'zero CLS by construction' for the in-flow strip (DRAFT.md:33-34), but the collapsed row's height now depends on a late-arriving webfont's metrics; a fallback→JetBrains Mono swap that changes line box height shifts the ENTIRE store document below the strip, on measured pages, during measured runs. (2) ADR-0001 addendum F (0001:183-186): the chrome's runtime cost 'is measured once (with/without batch, one profile) and published as a stated constant — byte-stripping alone does not remove it from timing metrics'; adding a font download (plus a much larger panel DOM) grows that constant, and the draft never schedules the re-measure. Current chrome.css deliberately ships no fonts (packages/switcher/src/chrome.css:1-6).
findings.why_it_matters: Instrumentation-induced CLS lands identically on every variant (fair) but destroys the 'zero CLS by construction' headline and hands a skeptic 'the benchmark site's own pages shift'.
findings.fix: Pin the collapsed row's block-size in font-independent units (or use `size-adjust`/metric-override fallback so swap is reflow-free), `font-display: swap` with matched metrics; and record in the ADR that the addendum-F with/without-chrome batch re-runs after the redesign, with the new constant published.

findings.claim_attacked: Collapsed-row live mini-readout PLUS the expanded panel's five-vital 'Your visit' — two sets of data-pm-hud-live slots (DRAFT.md:65, 71, 92-94)
findings.evidence: packages/measurement/src/client.ts:37 — `const slot = chrome?.querySelector(`[data-pm-hud-live="${metric.name}"]`)` — querySelector (singular) writes the FIRST matching slot only. With the mini-readout first in DOM order, the panel's LCP/CLS slots stay em-dash forever while the collapsed row updates (or vice versa).
findings.why_it_matters: The expanded instrument would visibly show stale/empty vitals next to a live collapsed row — on the surface whose whole job is measurement credibility. Fixing it means changing measure.js, which is 'the one ruler' (ADR-0001 §2) injected everywhere and used by the bench harness.
findings.fix: Either give the mini-readout distinct hooks (e.g. data-pm-hud-live-mini) mirrored by a small extension, or switch client.ts to querySelectorAll — in both cases name the measure.js change in the draft and fold it into the addendum-F chrome-constant re-measure.

findings.claim_attacked: 'Mechanics preserved' lists only `nav#pm-chrome` id + data-pm-hud-live hooks — omitting the rest of the load-bearing marker/dataset contract, and the origin-suite assertions the new anatomy breaks (DRAFT.md:117-125)
findings.evidence:
Beacon tags are read off the #pm-chrome dataset (packages/measurement/src/client.ts:20-26: data-pm-variant/surface/environment/cache-state/location) and asserted end-to-end (tools/origin-suite/suite/chrome.browser.test.ts:75-81). Assertions the redesign breaks or silently depends on, none named by the draft: chrome.test.ts:19/:70 (`data-pm-chrome="1"` exactly once); chrome.test.ts:40-50 (the byte-identical check strips a FLAT `
…
` via a non-greedy regex to the first `` — a details/summary panel or any nested div inside the switcher row poisons the strip and the cell extraction); chrome.test.ts:139-141 (hud region regex + EXACTLY one aria-current — the profile selector moves into panel section 4 and the collapsed row adds a variant aria-current); chrome.test.ts:107 (`count(body, "
findings.why_it_matters: The dataset contract is the beacon pipeline; dropping an attribute the draft never names ships 'unknown'-tagged RUM. And the suite-update surface is real work the session plan should size.
findings.fix: Extend §1.4's preserved-mechanics list to the full contract (data-pm-chrome, the five dataset tags, data-pm-switcher/data-pm-hud/data-pm-hud-lab or their successors) and enumerate the chrome.test.ts / chrome.browser.test.ts rewrites as an explicit deliverable of the chrome build.

findings.claim_attacked: Checkout's 'simulated — no payment, nothing ships, no PII' notice listed as a `pm-plaque--fenced` user, whose variant 'adds… the "EXCLUDED FROM EVERY BENCHMARK NUMBER" rule line' (DRAFT.md:133-138)
findings.evidence: Checkout is a measured spotlight surface: ADR-0001 addendum B (0001:149-158) — 'The Checkout cells therefore publish lab INP (scripted)'; decision-map.md:32 spotlights 'INP under load'. The fence semantics are reserved for number-excluded exhibits (remix3-frontier finding 5, decision-map.md:178: `data-pm-fenced="true"` + 'excluded from every benchmark number'; CONTEXT.md live-origin/misapplication definitions). A fenced plaque on the checkout form asserts the surface's numbers don't exist — while the chrome one panel up publishes its INP row.
findings.why_it_matters: A skeptic reading 'EXCLUDED FROM EVERY BENCHMARK NUMBER' above the card fields concludes checkout isn't benchmarked — directly contradicting the surface's published INP story, on-page.
findings.fix: The checkout simulation notice is a base `pm-plaque` (claim: simulated commerce, no PII), not `--fenced`. Reserve `--fenced` for the four true number-exclusions (Remix 3, Apollo exhibit, live-origin demonstration, and — vacuously but harmlessly — the a11y DS-OFF exhibits).

findings.claim_attacked: The lab table + 'When a number lands here it carries its receipt… or it doesn't land at all' empty state rendering on ALL surfaces, singletons included (DRAFT.md:76-85, 68-69)
findings.evidence: ADR-0007 §5 (0007:99-101): the home lab panel is omitted 'because no lab snapshot will ever exist for a surface off the benchmarked matrix.' The a11y section and how-it-was-built are singletons off the matrix (CONTEXT.md:29-33; decision-map.md:33-34). The draft differentiates singletons only in the collapsed row ('show the surface name instead of a switcher', DRAFT.md:68-69); the reading table + 'when a number lands here' line renders everywhere.
findings.why_it_matters: On singleton surfaces the empty state promises numbers that are never coming — a C2 phrasing-discipline failure by ADR-0007's own reasoning, on the instrument itself.
findings.fix: Branch the reading section on matrix membership: matrix surfaces get the sparse table + receipt empty state; singletons get home's plain sentence (no lab panel will exist for this surface — here's why), reusing ADR-0007 §5's precedent.

findings.claim_attacked: 'Checkout: profile selector foregrounded (CPU is the axis)' as the fulfillment of ADR-0004 §7's 'device/CPU controls on Checkout' (DRAFT.md:103-104)
findings.evidence: The profile selector is the constant HUD control every surface already owns (ADR-0005 §8, 0005:156-159: 'the profile selector the HUD already owns'), and ADR-0004 §6 makes it a snapshot selector that never re-throttles (0004:121-129). So 'foregrounded' leaves Checkout with an empty contextual control-set — §7's 'device/CPU controls on Checkout' (0004:143) reduced to reordering a control that exists everywhere. §6 names the only honest live form such a control could take: 'A synthetic throttle survives only as a clearly-fenced "feel the difference" demo, never fed into a number' (0004:128-129). Not owed (permissive wording), but the draft neither ships it nor records the descoping.
findings.why_it_matters: The per-surface contextual-switcher story ('the control-set is a function of the surface') is one of ADR-0004's headline decisions; quietly hollowing one of its four named allocations weakens it without a recorded reason.
findings.fix: Decide explicitly: either add a fenced in-page CPU 'feel the difference' demo to Checkout (plaqued `--fenced`, per §6's carve-out) or record in the new ADR that Checkout's contextual controls collapse to profile-foregrounding and why (the honest device/CPU axis lives only in the lab profiles).

findings.claim_attacked: 'Thumbnails reuse the gallery images (same URLs — browser cache, zero extra bytes)' (DRAFT.md:300-302)
findings.evidence: The thumbnail row renders up to 5 buttons above the fold (DRAFT.md:171-173). Reusing gallery URLs means up to 4 NOT-yet-viewed 600px AVIFs (~25-28 KB each per images-index.json `bytes`; e.g. 27,689 / 28,417 for release 10098948) download eagerly to paint ~64-80 CSS px thumbs — roughly +100 KB of image bytes on the measured PDP, competing with the fetchpriority-high LCP figure on the slow-4G profile. 'Zero extra bytes' is only true retrospectively, for a visitor who eventually views all five full images. The alternative is cheap: issue #9 comment (follow-up 6) — 'originals are retained, so re-derivation never touches Discogs' — a thumb derivative set costs one local re-derivation.
findings.why_it_matters: Kill-condition 1 (DRAFT.md:14-19) is exactly this: 600px assets as thumbnails is an engineering choice a skeptic can blame for the gap instead of the paradigm.
findings.fix: Either mint a small thumb derivative (from retained originals; update images-index) or make the honest tradeoff explicit in §4.2 (eager 2×-gallery preload as a deliberate choice, with the byte figure) — and state the thumbs' loading attributes either way.

findings.claim_attacked: Buy panel designed as format-switch radio group + price + numForSale + quantity, with no degenerate states (DRAFT.md:176-183)
findings.evidence: The frozen crate mostly lacks the data this design assumes. jq over crate/details.json: 439/500 releases have exactly ONE `formats[]` entry (the capture was vinyl-only by spec, issue #9), so the 'format switch (radio group segmented control — the release's formats[])' is a one-option radio on 88% of PDPs — including the draft's own featured release (Refinement of the Decline: formats = [Vinyl 3LP], one entry). And 44/500 releases have `priceFrom: null` + `numForSale: 0` — the price line, stock line, quantity, and Add-to-crate CTA have no defined state for them.
findings.why_it_matters: The format switch is named Product interactivity (CONTEXT.md:94-97) backing the render-axis thesis; a single-segment control is no interaction to measure and odd UI. The canonical markup contract must define both shapes or variants will improvise divergently and fail the drift gate.
findings.fix: Specify the n=1 formats state (static meta line, no radio group) and the unpriced/zero-stock state (CTA disabled or 'currently unavailable' line) in the canonical markup contract; pick the reference-render PDP release deliberately (a multi-format, priced one) so the golden master exercises the rich path.

findings.claim_attacked: Fixed 1:1 aspect frame with object-fit: contain letterboxing as the CLS answer, with the rejected per-release reservation dismissed unargued (DRAFT.md:187-194)
findings.evidence: First-image aspect ratios in the crate run 0.75 (portrait) to 2.01 (a 2:1 landscape/gatefold) — jq over details.json; 454/500 are within 2% of square but the extremes letterbox to half the mat's height. The alternative the draft raises and drops ('the gallery column reserves max(image heights)?') is fully available under the zero-bias rules: every image's width/height already rides as data (issue #9 AC; release-card.css:37-38 precedent 'Dimensions come from DATA'), so the frame can be sized per RELEASE (max over its ≤5 images at column width) server-side, identically in every paradigm — zero CLS on thumbnail switching AND minimal letterbox. The draft invites this attack explicitly.
findings.why_it_matters: The 1:1 frame is defended with the falsified 1,022 statistic (see the §4.2 kill); with the real numbers (296/500 first images exactly square) the choice may still win — squares are the record-store norm — but it currently rests on wrong data and an unargued rejection.
findings.fix: Argue both options with the true distribution: fixed 1:1 mat (uniform gallery geometry, worst case ~50% letterbox on 2:1 covers) vs per-release max-height frame (data-driven, no letterbox, geometry varies per PDP). Record the pick and its tradeoff in the ADR.

findings.claim_attacked: Store brand 'Matrix Records' justified as 'the metaphor spent on the right axis (the pressing identifies the variant)' (DRAFT.md:150-152)
findings.evidence: ADR-0007 §4 (0007:86-88) spent this exact distinction the other way: 'matrix numbers belong to pressings/variants, catalogue numbers to a label's listing — the metaphor spent on the right axis'. The store is the label/catalogue side of that mapping; naming the STORE 'Matrix' puts the pressing-side word on the label-side entity — the inversion ADR-0007 §4's considered-alternatives explicitly rejected for the gateway rows. It also collapses the two-register separation: the specimen (store fiction) now carries the instrument's (project's) name.
findings.why_it_matters: The metaphor discipline is a recorded decision with reasoning; the brand contradicts its own cited justification.
findings.fix: Pick a fiction-side name from the crate's world (a plausible label-shop name), or if 'Matrix Records' stays, rewrite the justification honestly (brand recall trumps axis purity) instead of citing the axis argument it inverts.

findings.claim_attacked: 'Add-to-crate' as the cart CTA (DRAFT.md:157-158, 181-182)
findings.evidence: CONTEXT.md:51-54 reserves **Crate** for 'the curated snapshot scope — one coherent slice of records'; the cart is separately defined as application state (CONTEXT.md:197-199). The draft itself uses 'a crate cover' (snapshot sense, line 156) and 'Add-to-crate CTA (cart state)' (cart sense, line 157-158) in adjacent lines.
findings.why_it_matters: Every future ADR, registry id (`checkout-*`, cart interactions), and panel discussion now has two meanings of the project's most-used noun.
findings.fix: Rename the CTA in the store fiction ('Add to bag', 'Add to cart') or, if the crate-digging pun is wanted, add an explicit CONTEXT.md entry disambiguating store-crate (cart) from the frozen crate — the ubiquitous language file must not silently fork.

findings.claim_attacked: 'Recorded in coverage.json as a decided fallback list (renamed from "deferred")' + fontkit guard hardening, without naming the test churn or the Inter-coverage receipt (DRAFT.md:288-295)
findings.evidence: The counts check out (verified: coverage.json `crateDeferredI18n` = 30 codepoints = the draft's 13 Latin-adjacent + 8 Arabic + 9 CJK), and the split is within the crate-glyph-coverage ticket's decision space (decision-map.md:191: 'or accept a documented per-OS fallback for rare scripts'). But the rename touches tools/repo-checks/test/font-covers-crate.test.ts:44 (interface key), :84, :115 (usages) and the 'deferred i18n' copy at :105-108/:111 — and the :111-117 'stays minimal' test will automatically force the 13 newly-covered codepoints OFF the list once PM Crate Symbols lands (works in the draft's favor; worth stating). 'the Latin-adjacent deferred set that Inter covers' is asserted, not receipted — the build must verify all 13 against Inter v4.1's cmap with fontTools before the ADR records it.
findings.why_it_matters: Small, but the draft claims the guard 'can no longer be hand-faked' while not naming the guard edits its own rename requires.
findings.fix: List the test-file changes as build deliverables; add 'fontTools-verify Inter covers the 13' as an acceptance criterion; note the stays-minimal test enforces the list migration automatically.

findings.claim_attacked: The bench-strip register 'extends to every surface' with no statement about home's own in-page HUD (DRAFT.md:30-34, 117-120)
findings.evidence: Home renders its own HUD from page-inlined CSS, not /_pm/chrome.css (ADR-0007 §5-§6; chrome.test.ts:76-86 asserts home has `id="pm-chrome"` but no injected chrome). After the redesign, home's band and the variants' strip share the id + measure.js contract but not the new anatomy/typeface — two instrument identities on adjacent pages.
findings.why_it_matters: The instrument register is the draft's hero; visible divergence between the front door's band and every other page's strip reads as drift on the one element claimed to be constant.
findings.fix: State the decision: home's band adopts the collapsed-row look (a home rebuild task) or deliberately keeps its wider ADR-0007 composition — recorded either way.

findings.claim_attacked: How-it-was-built gets a doc layout but no URL/registration decision, and the a11y section is unreachable from the shell (DRAFT.md:144-148, 255-261)
findings.evidence: ADR-0004 §3 path scheme is /{variant}/{surface}/; the a11y section resolves this (/vanilla/a11y/, DRAFT.md:239) but §2.6 never places how-it-was-built (decision-map.md:34 calls it a singleton whose form is 'store chrome, editorial' — the draft's TOC-doc layout departs from that row unrecorded). The shell's masthead lists Records/Editorial/Checkout and its footer 'what is this? · how it was built · GitHub' (DRAFT.md:146-148) — the a11y section appears in neither; only home's gateway links it.
findings.why_it_matters: Chrome registration (§4.4) needs surface keys, so the how-built path is load-bearing; and a spotlight surface with no in-store route contradicts solo-first discoverability.
findings.fix: Decide how-built's path (and record the departure from the decision-map row's 'store chrome, editorial' form); add the a11y section to the footer link set.

findings.claim_attacked: Editorial prose built around '$515.24' and a dated byline, under §4.3's rule 'Numbers/dates in any render come from tray/manifest fields — never typed' (DRAFT.md:161-166, 312-314)
findings.evidence: The $515.24 figure is real (verified: max priceFrom in summaries.json = 515.24, Stars Of The Lid, 'And Their Refinement Of The Decline', 2007, Kranky — draft facts all check out) but the essay is hand-written copy; if the price appears in prose as typed text it is exempt from §4.3's own mechanism and goes stale on any re-freeze. The byline date has no tray/manifest source at all.
findings.why_it_matters: §4.3 states the rule as 'now structural for all six surfaces'; Editorial is the surface where it's least structural as drafted.
findings.fix: Render the price via the featured-release card (which reads the tray) and keep the typed prose allusive ('north of five hundred dollars' — or interpolate the tray field through the reference renderer); define the byline date's source (fiction constant, exempted explicitly, or manifest date).

survived: §1.1 bench-strip placement + native <details> panel: anchors-only core, in-flow injection, and the unchanged div#pm-chrome-slot contract keep the drift normalizer (normalize.ts:107) and gate slot-removal (gate.ts:79) working untouched; the rejected-placement reasoning is sound.

§1.2 chrome-owned mono AUTHORIZATION: ADR-0006's alternatives explicitly reserve the mono 'through the --pm-font-metric slot and chrome CSS' (0006:129-132) and ADR-0003 §8 itself names mono figures for the HUD — the draft's quote is accurate and --pm-font-metric staying untouched is the right read of 'one face for UI and metrics'.

§1.3 empty states' C2 compliance on matrix surfaces: the new lab copy keeps the exact 'No published runs yet' prefix (chrome.test.ts:138/:145 stay green) and the verdict slot's 'Indistinguishable at this sample size' matches ADR-0001 addendum C verbatim.

§1.3.5 PLP slots-without-wiring: ADR-0005's consequences assign the §8 HUD additions to the downstream PLP build ticket, and §4.4's sparse registration means no 404 preset anchors can render before a variant serves the surface — shipping labeled slots now is consistent, not a violation.

§4.1 glyph split arithmetic and legitimacy: 13 + 17 = 30 verified against coverage.json crateDeferredI18n; per-OS fallback for Arabic/CJK is inside the crate-glyph-coverage ticket's recorded decision space, preserves cross-variant zero-bias (the OS fallback is constant across variants on any given machine), and the fontkit hardening matches the ticket's own recorded future hardening.

§4.3 built reference renders: the no-component-runtime guard checks package.json entry fields only (no-component-runtime.test.ts:40-57) — a build script violates neither its letter nor its spirit; reference.test.ts's script-free and asset-resolution assertions hold on committed built masters; CI-builds-from-fixture honors ADR-0007's crate rule.
§2.1 factual spine: Stars Of The Lid / 'And Their Refinement Of The Decline' / 2007 / Kranky / $515.24 all verified against the committed trays (it is the crate's maximum priceFrom).
§2.4 checkout INP story: real-work-only interactions (no busy loops), 'INP (scripted)' labeling, and registry-id receipts match ADR-0001 addendum B exactly.
§4.2's PLP-card leg: pm-grid minmax(220px,1fr) (release-card.css:16) puts cards at ~220-300 CSS px, so the 600px derivative ≈ the 2× asset — that half of the sizing rationale is data-supported.
